mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP growth and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust corporate revenue expansion without margin compression, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative returns in greater than 50% of cases when both series trend upward concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and inventory restocking, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 during documented recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated policy rates combined with consecutive declines in total nonfarm payrolls indicate monetary tightening is successfully suppressing economic activity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and PAYEMS declines for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when unemployment remains below 4.0% across all tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in advanced retail sales directly correlates with consumer expenditure strength, which constitutes the largest component of corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all rolling 3-year windows regardless of macroeconomic conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising unemployment confirms a broad-based economic slowdown, reducing corporate profit expectations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of instances when both indicators move in opposing directions.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price levels alongside robust housing construction activity signal sustained aggregate demand and economic resilience, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns turn negative in greater than 60% of triggered observations during high-inflation periods.
